# type-is

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Infer the content-type of a request.

This module is used within [Express](https://expressjs.com/) and [body-parser](https://www.npmjs.com/package/body-parser) to determine the content-type of incoming requests, making it a critical component for proper request handling and body parsing in Express.js applications.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install type-is
```

## API

```js
var typeIs = require('type-is')
```

### typeIs(request, types)

This function is a shorthand for `typeIs.is(req.headers['content-type'], types)`.

```js
var is = typeIs(req, ['json', 'urlencoded', 'multipart'])
```

- `request` - Node.js HTTP request object
- `types` - Array of types to check

The request is checked for a content-type. If the request has no body, `null` is returned. If the request has no content type, `false` is returned. Otherwise, the first `type` that matches is returned, or `false` if no match is found.

### typeIs.is(mediaType, types)

Compare a content-type against a list of types.

```js
var mediaType = 'application/json'
var is = typeIs.is(mediaType, ['json', 'urlencoded', 'multipart'])
// => 'json'
```

- `mediaType` - Content-type value from request
- `types` - Array of types to check

Each type can be:
- A simple MIME type string (like `json`, `text/html`, `application/json`)
- A MIME type with wildcards (like `*/*`, `*/json`, `application/*`)
- A suffix preceded by `+` (like `+json`)

If no types match, `false` is returned. Otherwise, the first matching type is returned.

### typeIs.hasBody(request)

Returns a Boolean indicating if the request has a body.

```js
if (typeIs.hasBody(req)) {
  // process request body
}
```

A request with a body must have either a `transfer-encoding` or `content-length` header.

## Examples

### Within Express

This module is already included within Express as part of the `req.is()` method:

```js
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

app.post('/api/users', function (req, res, next) {
  if (!req.is('json')) {
    return res.status(415).send('Unsupported Media Type')
  }
  
  // process JSON request body
  next()
})
```

### With body-parser

This module is used by body-parser to determine which parser to use:

```js
var bodyParser = require('body-parser')
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

// parse json requests
app.use(bodyParser.json())

// parse urlencoded requests
app.use(bodyParser.urlencoded({ extended: false }))

app.post('/api/users', function (req, res) {
  // req.body will be parsed based on Content-Type
  res.send('User created')
})
```

### Direct usage

```js
var http = require('http')
var typeIs = require('type-is')

var server = http.createServer(function (req, res) {
  var isJson = typeIs(req, ['json'])
  
  if (isJson) {
    // process JSON request body
    res.end('JSON request')
    return
  }
  
  res.end('Not a JSON request')
})

server.listen(3000)
```
